text: That man's name is Bob Pettit. He never had a season averaging under 20 PPG, nor a season averaging less than 12 RPG. He also won 2 scoring titles, rookie of the year, and a rebounding title. He even served as a player-coach at one point. He was the first player in league history to pass 20,000 career points, and all these he's still top 50, even with playing only 11 seasons. He's third all time in career rebounds per game after only the great Wilt Chamberlain and Bill Russell, and top 10 in points per game.
label: Analysis

text: Think about Kobe or MJ. Would either of these players have cared a fig about efficiency? No. They were going to shoot and and shoot until they won the game, and they didn't give a rat's ass about getting other players involved.I feel like today's players are in love with chasing efficiency. They'd rather have impressive 50-40-90 statlines than win games. It's a loser's mentality. Kawhi, KD, Jokic, all will sometimes give up hard shots in order to chase efficiency.That's why I love watching Ant, Brunson or Luka. They can brick shot after shot after shot and still keep on shooting, bc they know eventually that shot will fall. They play hero ball in the clutch. They don't care about efficiency.
label: Analysis

text: I just read an article on a way to fix tanking that does a regression based on EPM wins above replacement. Where the value V = 1 - 0.2490 * ln(x) where x is the pick number. Not sure that is the perfect methodology but its at least a start. Sourced from https://www.natesilver.net/p/radical-plan-to-replace-the-nba-draft-lottery-arc-auction
label: Analysis

text: I judged a lot of players based on stats when I was younger, but as I've gotten older I've realized that the context matters so much more. In an extreme example, I rewatched a condensed version of Luka's 73 point game against the Hawks after Bam's 83 point game because someone said it was way more impressive, and it is. Beyond the amazing shooting performance (I think Luka was 25/32), it was a close game and the Mavs absolutely had to have 73 from Luka to win. The Heat were intentionally fouling to get Bam a number.
label: Analysis
